description_long: This object consists of a group of utility poles, each constructed from a tall, cylindrical wooden shaft with a weathered brown finish. At the top of each pole, there are horizontal crossarms supporting multiple insulators, which are used to hold electrical wires. Some of the poles are equipped with large, cylindrical electrical transformers mounted midway up the shaft, while others have attached metal fixtures such as ladders or signage. The bases of the poles are slightly flared for stability, and the overall design is typical of those found along urban streets or in residential neighborhoods. The visible hardware includes metal brackets, insulators, and a variety of attached electrical components.
description: A group of weathered wooden utility poles with crossarms, insulators, transformers, and various metal fixtures, typical for urban electrical infrastructure.
description_view_0: Front view showing all utility poles with visible crossarms, insulators, transformers, and attached fixtures such as ladders and signage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the poles, with transformers and crossarms visible in profile and the attached fixtures extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the utility poles, with crossarms, insulators, and transformers visible, as well as the arrangement of attached fixtures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the slim profile of the poles and the side-mounted fixtures, with transformers and crossarms in profile.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Each pole has a single horizontal crossarm mounted near the top, with some poles featuring additional fixtures or equipment below.
